mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often preceded by UNRATE spikes
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with stable, low inflation indicates healthy consumer demand without overheating, supporting corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth < 3%
failure_condition: If the combination of strong retail sales and low inflation does not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery indicates increasing manufacturing activity and business investment, signaling a broader economic rebound that drives corporate earnings and S&P 500 appreciation over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:US economy emerging from recession in 2009
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 5% annualized during recovery periods does not correlate with positive S&P 500 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts reflects growing confidence in the housing market and broader economy, stimulating construction-related industries and consumer wealth, which supports short-term S&P 500 gains over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST month-over-month growth exceeding 10% fails to predict positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, reducing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, stimulating investment and consumption, which supports equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate revenues and profits across various sectors, which fundamentally supports higher equity prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the annualized quarter-over-quarter growth rate of GDPC1 shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining unemployment and increasing payrolls indicates a robust and improving labor market, signaling strong economic momentum that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods where both UNRATE declines and PAYEMS increases as specified do not result in positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 65% of cases.
